description:
You are acting as an MLOps engineer who wants to log the very latest training-run metrics in a compact, key-value format that another system will later scrape.

    1. A CSV file already exists at  
       /home/user/experiments/exp_v1/metrics.csv  
       and contains the experiment’s historical metrics (header on the first
       line, then one line per epoch).

    2. Your job is to extract ONLY the last (i.e., most recent) data row from
       that CSV file and write it to a new file located at  
       /home/user/experiments/exp_v1/latest_metrics.log

    3. The new file must contain exactly one line—no header, no leading or
       trailing blank lines, and no spaces.  The required format is:

       epoch=<integer>;loss=<float>;accuracy=<float>

       For example, if the last CSV row were
       7,0.123,0.987
       then latest_metrics.log must contain precisely
       epoch=7;loss=0.123;accuracy=0.987

    4. If /home/user/experiments/exp_v1/latest_metrics.log already exists,
       overwrite it completely.

    5. Only standard UNIX tools that are usually available in a minimal Linux
       environment may be used.  A single terminal command is sufficient.

    Verification notes (important):
    • The automated test will check that
      a) /home/user/experiments/exp_v1/latest_metrics.log exists,
      b) it contains exactly one non-blank line,
      c) the line’s three key names (epoch, loss, accuracy) and delimiters
         (“=” between key and value, “;” between pairs) appear exactly as
         specified, and
      d) the values match the very last row of the CSV file.

    Please perform the required transformation and create the log file.
truth:
Initial filesystem state before the agent starts:

    Path: /home/user/experiments/exp_v1/
    ├── metrics.csv
    └── (latest_metrics.log does NOT exist yet)

    Contents of /home/user/experiments/exp_v1/metrics.csv

        epoch,loss,accuracy
        1,0.678,0.75
        2,0.432,0.82
        3,0.321,0.86

    Because the last line is “3,0.321,0.86”, the correct target file
    (/home/user/experiments/exp_v1/latest_metrics.log) must end up containing
    exactly the following single line (no extra spaces or trailing newline
    characters beyond the usual single LF):

        epoch=3;loss=0.321;accuracy=0.86
name: task_000000_6e327d30
